acceptance: A single Boost.Asio runtime owner exists for timers, posted wakeups, cancellation, and shutdown.
verification: `cmake --build --preset default` linked aria2-next and aria2_tests with AsioRuntime in cmake/Sources.cmake; `build/default/aria2_tests 'All Tests/aria2::DownloadEngineTest'` passed.
notes: Initial integration bridges to the old command loop while protocol migration proceeds.

acceptance: Timer and wakeup behavior can be driven through the runtime boundary without busy loops.
verification: Focused tests cover posted task wake, timer wake, and wake consumption. `git diff --check` passed.
notes: TimeBasedCommand still exists until protocol users migrate; wake requests are consumed after forcing one non-blocking poll.

acceptance: Signal handling enters the same shutdown path as existing DownloadEngine halt behavior.
verification: Focused halt test verifies `requestHalt()` sets halt state and wakes the runtime. Existing global signal flag path remains unchanged.
notes: Force halt also wakes the runtime. Full signal replacement is deferred until the old command loop is retired.

target_files: src/EventPoll.*; src/EpollEventPoll.*; src/PollEventPoll.*; src/KqueueEventPoll.*; src/SelectEventPoll.*; src/PortEventPoll.*; src/LibuvEventPoll.*
